items.title: Deep Learning — Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville (MIT Press)
items.author: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville
items.note: Chapter 6 introduces feedforward networks, hidden layers, and how depth with nonlinearities enables complex input-output mappings.

items.title: Neural Networks and Deep Learning — Michael Nielsen (online book)
items.author: Michael Nielsen
items.note: Chapter 4 walks through visual intuition for hidden neurons and why stacked layers compute non-trivial functions including XOR.

items.title: Learning representations by back-propagating errors — Nature (1986)
items.author: David E. Rumelhart, Geoffrey E. Hinton, Ronald J. Williams
items.note: Seminal paper demonstrating practical training of multilayer networks with hidden units — historical foundation for the modern MLP.

items.title: Perceptrons — Minsky & Papert (MIT Press, 1969)
items.author: Marvin Minsky, Seymour Papert
items.note: Documents limitations of single-layer perceptrons including XOR — motivates why multilayer architectures with hidden units were needed.
